title: Risk Index Spike for Privileged Accounts

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF DE.AE-03 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF DE.AE-03 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.38: Risk Index Spike for Privileged Accounts.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.38' running on from datamodel Risk.All_Risk, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk earliest=-7d
| search risk_object_type=user risk_object=*admin*
| stats sum(risk_score) as score by risk_object
| where score>50
description: Surfaces elevated Splunk ES risk scores for privileged users for layered adverse event analysis.

Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `from datamodel Risk.All_Risk`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Calibrate thresholds; (2) enrich with geo and MFA; (3) pair with analyst workflow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| from datamodel Risk.All_Risk earliest=-7d
| search risk_object_type=user risk_object=*admin*
| stats sum(risk_score) as score by risk_object
| where score>50
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Index Spike for Privileged Accounts (DE.AE-03)** — Surfaces elevated Splunk ES risk scores for privileged users for layered adverse event analysis.

Documented **Data sources**: `from datamodel Risk.All_Risk`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `from` (dataset / Federated Search) — verify dataset availability and permissions.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by risk_object** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where score>50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Scatter
grandmaExplanation: We watch for surfaces elevated splunk es risk scores for privileged users for layered adverse event analysis. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
